events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: crew proposes a shortcut for torque & threaded fastening, but the submittal omits the product listing page and project note that would decide whether the shortcut is allowed. Reference material on hand: OEM/engineering specs.

The work is in-progress. The shortcut's permissibility depends on missing documentation (product listing page and project note). We have OEM/engineering specs but not the submittal documents that would decide whether the shortcut is allowed.

Decision: needs_more_info — because the shortcut's validity depends on omitted documentation. Risk: medium (torque fastening shortcuts can affect joint integrity). Work stage: in-progress. Percent complete: maybe 50? Not specified. I'll estimate based on in-progress. Let's say 40.

Component conditions: none flagged — empty list.

Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked).

Confidence: maybe 0.7.

Findings: proposed shortcut lacks required submittal documentation to verify compliance; product listing page and project note omitted.

Actions: halt shortcut pending submission and review of product listing page and project note; verify against OEM/engineering specs; obtain engineering approval before proceeding.

Rationale: shortcut cannot be approved without the documentation that determines whether it's allowed; OEM specs alone may not cover project-specific allowances.

References: OEM/engineering specs.
